title: Clavicular Frame Mogged by ASU Frat Leader
about:
Clavicular Frame Mogged by ASU Frat Leader refers to a series of memes based on the caption of a viral tweet attached to a video of Kick streamer and looksmaxxer Clavicular posing for a photo with a muscular Arizona State University (ASU) "frat leader," Varis Gilaj, reading, "Clavicular ran into a frat leader at ASU and got brutally frame mogged by him." The post was made on February 6th, 2026, by X / Twitter user @biggerboy111 and became the subject of reactions and memes over the following days due to the use of the slang term "frame mogged," meaning the ASU frat leader's build was more impressive than Clavicular's.

At the time of the meme's popularity, similar posts featuring slang overload captions alongside clips of Clavicular also went viral, such as a post about Clavicular jestermaxxing. These posts inspired parody memes. The so-called ASU frat leader is TikToker @v.varis, also known as Varis.
origin:
On February 5th, 2026, Clavicular hosted an IRL Kick [1] stream from ASU. During the stream, he attended a frat party. About three hours into the stream, Clavicular goes into a frat house to try and briefly get away from the crowd. At the 3:08:00 timestamp, Clavicular takes a selfie with a "frat leader," a muscular guy in a muscle shirt. Clavicular comments, "You got me by a lot, I stopped gyming." [2]

The man who "frame mogs" Clavicular is known online as Varis, or @v.varis on TikTok. [3] Getting "frame mogged" means that someone near you has a better "frame" or "build" than you.

On February 6th, X [4] user @biggerboy111 posted the clip of Clavicular meeting the ASU frat leader, writing, "Clavicular ran into a frat leader at ASU and got brutally frame mogged by him👀😂," garnering over 18,000 likes, 1,600 reposts and 13.5 million views in three days.
